mappings.rationaleAI: Cost analysis addresses production costs in short run (A.1) and long run (A.2). Market structures address distribution (B). Labour markets address labour input (C.1).
Resource allocation addresses production (A) and distribution (B). Division of labour addresses inputs (C). Market structures address money (D.1). International trade connects to F.
Work addresses labour input (C.1), workforce deployment (C.1.a), and employment (C.1.e).
HR practices address personnel administration (H.3), recruitment (H.3.b), training (H.3.c), and incentive (H.3.d).
Employment rights address pay rates (C.1.b) and pay structure (C.1.c).
Management theory addresses managers and entrepreneurs (C.1.j) and human resources (H.3).
Entrepreneurial processes address managers and entrepreneurs (C.1.j).
Organizational analysis addresses the business corporation (D.4) and production control (H.4).

mappings.vsiAuthor: John O. S. Wilson, John Goddard
mappings.rationaleAI: Banking addresses banking development (D.2.a), modern structures (D.2.b), commercial banking (D.2.c), and central banking (D.2.d).
Accounting addresses keeping accounts (H.1), information systems (H.1.a), financial statements (H.1.b), and measurement (H.1.c).
Asset valuation addresses the money market (D.2.e) and long-term funds (D.2.g). Capital structure relates to long-term operations (H.2.b).
Energy conversion addresses energy as input (C.3).
Renewable energy addresses energy as input (C.3).
Agricultural science addresses agricultural economics (E), technology effects (E.4), and farming organization (E.5).
Advertising industry addresses advertising (I).
Marketing addresses distribution (H.5), marketing departments (H.5.a), and market research (H.5.e).
WTO addresses trade theories (F.1), tariffs (F.2.a), and trade arrangements (F.3).
Tax economics addresses taxation in budgets (G.1), government resources (G.2), and spending growth (G.3).
Risk analysis addresses distribution of risk (J).
Statistical methods address economic statistics (D.3).
Innovation addresses entrepreneurs (C.1.j) and the business corporation (D.4).
IP law connects to trade-related intellectual property (F.2.a).
Competition law addresses factors influencing trade (F.2.a).
Global trade addresses geographical distribution of resources (F).
Industrialization addresses labour force (C.1.a), capital (C.4), and production conditions (F.2.b).
Monetary thought addresses money (D.1) and international monetary institutions (D.2.f).
Smith's economic thought addresses money functions (D.1.a) and trade theories (F.1).
Project management addresses production administration (H.4).
Capital markets address money (D.1) and the business corporation (D.4).
CSR addresses the business corporation (D.4) and human resources (H.3).

Covers cognitive biases and heuristics in economic decision-making, directly relevant to understanding imperfect knowledge on the part of buyers and factors influencing consumers' tastes and spending patterns.

Covers comparative analysis of global economic development including the roles of geography, globalization, technological change, and government policy, directly relevant to the geographical distribution of resources and markets and international trade patterns.

Covers welfare state systems including social insurance, healthcare, and unemployment protections, directly relevant to the growth in government social welfare expenditures and government-sponsored health insurance programs.

Covers rational choice theory including utility, expected value, marginal utility, and risk aversion, directly relevant to the analysis of costs and output and the production function in both short and long run.

Covers externalities, market failure, cost-benefit analysis, and carbon taxes applied to environmental protection, directly addressing economic relationships not susceptible to control by prices such as air pollution.

Covers corruption as a global phenomenon affecting economic institutions and its relationship to poverty and economic instability, relevant to the theory of public expenditures and problems of effective tax administration.

Covers Russian economic development including state-controlled industries, central planning, and the hybrid economy combining state economic control with market participation, directly relevant to government operation of basic industries.

Covers industrial and product design practices at major companies like Nokia, Ford, Sony, and Lego, including the relationship between form and function and consumer culture, relevant to marketing departments and market research techniques.
